title: Fabio Cannavaro names Uzbekistan squad | FIFA World Cup 2026
original_text:
Stars Khusanov, Shomurodov and Fayzullaev headline the Uzbekistan squad as the White Wolves gear up for a maiden FIFA World Cup tilt.
Fresh off a narrow loss to co-hosts Canada in Edmonton, head coach Fabio Cannavaro has unveiled the Uzbekistan squad set to feature at the FIFA World Cup 2026™.
Cannavaro twice trimmed an initial 40-strong selection, with defender Ibrokhimkhalil Yuldoshev missing out through injury while forward Alisher Odilov was among those who didn’t make the cut.
Explore hospitality packages for Uzbekistan's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matchesExplore hospitality packages for Uzbekistan's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matches
Uzbekistan's World Cup squadUzbekistan's World Cup squad
GoalkeepersGoalkeepers
Botirali Ergashev Abduvohid Nematov Utkir Yusupov
DefendersDefenders
Abdulla Abdullaev Khojiakbar Alijonov Rustam Ashurmatov Umar Eshmuradov Bekhruzjon Karimov Abdukodir Khusanov Sherzod Nasrullaev Farrukh Sayfiev Avazbek Ulmasaliyev Jakhongir Urozov
MidfieldersMidfielders
Sherzod Esanov Azizjon Ganiev Dostonbek Khamdamov Jamshid Iskanderov Akmal Mozgovoy Otabek Shukurov Odiljon Xamrobekov
ForwardsForwards
Azizbek Amonov Abbosbek Fayzullaev Jaloliddin Masharipov Eldor Shomurodov Igor Sergeev Oston Urunov
All squads are provisional until the final list of 26 players is announced by FIFA, following their submission by participating teams, on 2 June.
Khusanov and Shomurodov headline White Wolves packKhusanov and Shomurodov headline White Wolves pack
29 years after they played their first FIFA World Cup™ qualifier – a 1-0 win against Yemen in front of 28,000 fans in Sanaa – Uzbekistan are now less than a month away from making their first appearance at the global finals.
The intervening three decades have seen rapid growth, with the nation hosting continental and global tournaments and boasting a slew of stars now featuring at leading clubs abroad. Manchester City defender Abdukodir Khusanov and Türkiye-based duo Abbosbek Fayzullaev and Eldor Shomurodov headline the preliminary list, with highly-rated teenage right-back Bekhruz Karimov among those to have caught Cannavaro’s eye.
Prior to their departure for North America, the White Wolves played an intra-squad friendly at Bunyodkor Stadium last Friday, an event attended by the nation’s President, Shavkat Mirziyoyev.
Uzbekistan now face the Netherlands in their final pre-tournament fixture, before facing Colombia, in Mexico City, in their Group K opener on 17 June.

17 June: Uzbekistan v Colombia - Mexico City Stadium
23 June: Portugal v Uzbekistan - Houston Stadium
27 June: Congo DR v Uzbekistan - Atlanta Stadium
